name: pymatgen
description: Materials science toolkit. Crystal structures (CIF, POSCAR), phase diagrams, band structure, DOS, Materials Project integration, format conversion, for computational materials science.
capabilities: Read and write crystal structure files in CIF, POSCAR, XYZ, and 100+ formats
Analyze symmetry, space groups, coordination environments, and lattice parameters
Construct phase diagrams and assess thermodynamic stability
Access Materials Project database for materials data and structures
Generate surfaces, slabs, and analyze electronic properties
Set up calculations for VASP, Gaussian, Quantum ESPRESSO, and other codes
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: All 537 static findings are false positives. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown documentation syntax (code blocks, inline code formatting) as shell commands and cryptographic terms in documentation text as weak algorithms. The actual Python code uses secure patterns for API key management (environment variables) and performs legitimate materials science file I/O operations. No malicious code, data exfiltration, or security risks found.
valueStatement: Work with crystal structures and materials data. Convert file formats, compute phase diagrams, analyze symmetry, and access the Materials Project database for computational materials science research.
limitations: Requires pymatgen and mp-api packages for full functionality
Materials Project API access requires a valid API key
Some analysis features require additional dependencies

useCases.title: Research stable compounds
useCases.description: Build phase diagrams and identify stable materials using data from the Materials Project database.

useCases.title: Convert structure formats
useCases.description: Convert crystal structure files between CIF, POSCAR, XYZ and other formats for different simulation codes.

useCases.title: Analyze crystal symmetry
useCases.description: Determine space groups, symmetry operations, and coordination environments from structure files.

promptTemplates.title: Read structure file
promptTemplates.scenario: Load and analyze a crystal structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Read the structure from file 'POSCAR' and show its composition, lattice parameters, and space group.

promptTemplates.title: Convert format
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert 'structure.cif' to POSCAR format and save as 'converted.POSCAR'.

promptTemplates.title: Phase diagram
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a phase diagram for the Li-Fe-O system and identify stable compounds.

promptTemplates.title: Materials Project
promptTemplates.prompt: Search the Materials Project for stable perovskite structures with band gap between 1.5 and 3.0 eV.

bestPractices: Always use environment variables (MP_API_KEY) for API credentials, never hardcode keys
Validate structures after reading to check for overlapping atoms or unrealistic bond lengths
Use SpacegroupAnalyzer to reduce structures to primitive cells before analysis
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode API keys in scripts - use environment variables instead
Do not skip symmetry analysis when comparing structures - different space groups may look similar
Do not assume automatic format detection always works - specify format when unsure

faq.question: What file formats does pymatgen support?
faq.answer: Pymatgen supports 100+ formats including CIF, POSCAR, XYZ, PDB, JSON, YAML, and inputs/outputs for VASP, Gaussian, Quantum ESPRESSO, LAMMPS, and more.

faq.question: How do I get a Materials Project API key?
faq.answer: Create an account at https://next-gen.materialsproject.org/ and generate an API key from your dashboard. Set it as the MP_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Can pymatgen analyze molecular dynamics trajectories?
faq.answer: Yes, use Xdatcar to read VASP MD trajectories and DiffusionAnalyzer to compute diffusivity and conductivity from AIMD data.

faq.question: How do I generate a supercell?
faq.answer: Use SupercellTransformation with a scaling matrix, for example: [[2,0,0],[0,2,0],[0,0,2]] for a 2x2x2 supercell.

faq.question: Does pymatgen work with other simulation codes?
faq.answer: Yes, pymatgen integrates with VASP, Gaussian, Quantum ESPRESSO, LAMMPS, CP2K, ABINIT, and can convert to/from ASE format.

faq.question: How do I analyze coordination environments?
faq.answer: Use CrystalNN from pymatgen.analysis.local_env for general cases, or VoronoiNN for Voronoi-based neighbor finding.
